old_claim.note: Currency provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Trade-capital contracts in the Old Assyrian network used silver, tin, textiles, prices, and exchange values; shared metrology scaled the valuation and settlement of those commercial obligations without requiring coined currency.

source_shape.source_locator: Research notes describe Bronze Age eastern Mediterranean weights, a comparative sample across greater Mesopotamia, and weighing as evidence for economic interactions and high-value commodities.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The source documents standardized Bronze Age weights and weighing practice as commercial infrastructure for economic interaction and high-value commodities.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: This supports replacing coined currency with standardized_weights_and_measures because Old Assyrian silver, tin, textile, price, and exchange-value obligations were valued and settled before coined currency.

ontology_before: Merchant Partnership Contracts depended on currency, laundering later coinage into Bronze Age trade-capital contracts whose evidence predates standardized coinage.
ontology_after: Merchant Partnership Contracts depends on standardized_weights_and_measures as commercial scaling infrastructure for silver and commodity valuation, while coined currency is explicitly outside scope.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: merchant_partnership_contracts now depends on standardized_weights_and_measures and no longer depends on currency.
would_reject_if: The node is rescoped to later Classical monetary partnerships that specifically require coined currency.
A source shows Old Assyrian partnership contracts operated without weight-based silver or commodity valuation.
The standardized_weights_and_measures node is narrowed away from Mesopotamian or eastern Mediterranean commercial metrology.
short_reason: Bronze Age partnership contracts used metrology and silver valuation, not coined-currency infrastructure.
